scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: Meets all pass criteria: 8 business rules extracted with individual confidence reasoning, 5+ escalations serving as clarifying questions, 'demand' explicitly called out as undefined, no Given/When/Then acceptance criteria written (empty array), confidence.level is 30 (≤ 40), and no specific values are invented without flagging them as assumptions.

observations.text: 8 well-structured business rules extracted, each with invariant classification and justification — exceeds the 5-rule minimum
Demand measurement identified as completely undefined in escalations and low_confidence_areas — the first escalation is dedicated to this critical gap
acceptance_criteria array is empty — correctly refused to write Given/When/Then from vague input
confidence.level of 30 appropriately reflects the massive ambiguity in the input

5 escalations with structured options and recommendations effectively serve as clarifying questions covering demand signal, guardrail thresholds, regulatory risk, competitive vs profitable tension, and quote validity
Where values are suggested (e.g., '≤ 60 seconds', '0.5x to 3.0x', '5-15 minutes'), they appear in recommendations/options within escalations, clearly framed as suggestions rather than requirements
Identified non-obvious implied rules like BR-5 (positive price), BR-6 (monotonic pricing), and BR-8 (quote honoring) — going beyond surface reading
Edge cases include regulatory price gouging risk and demand signal failure — demonstrates deep domain thinking

Some testable_assertions in business rules read like proto-acceptance-criteria (e.g., BR-6's mathematical formulation), though they stop short of full Given/When/Then format — borderline but acceptable
Assumptions are explicitly listed and flagged as assumed rather than stated — transparent about what was inferred vs specified

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'ambiguity', got values: 'missing_context', 'missing_context'
